title: Hanna-Mari Manninen featured as Europe Green Ambassadors 2026
content:
We’re delighted to share that Partner Hanna-Mari Manninen has been featured as one of Europe’s Green Ambassadors 2026 by Legal 500, marking a significant recognition of her expertise and commitment to sustainability in legal practice.

Hanna-Mari Manninen, a sustainability, ESG, and corporate law expert, heads the Strategic Advisory team at Dittmar & Indrenius, bringing a keen focus on the integration of green solutions within corporate strategy. Read more here.

The Green Ambassadors series by Legal 500 highlights private practice lawyers who champion sustainability and the green transition in their client work and beyond.

The post Hanna-Mari Manninen featured as Europe Green Ambassadors 2026 appeared first on Dittmar & Indrenius Attorneys Ltd..

title: Evästeet ja seurantateknologiat markkinoinnissa – Yliopiston Apteekin tapaus muistuttaa yrityksiä tietosuojan tärkeydestä evästeitä käytettäessä
content:
Helsingin hallinto-oikeus antoi 1.6.2026 päätöksen Yliopiston Apteekin tietosuoja-asiassa. Vaikka uutisointi keskittyi suurelta osin seuraamusmaksun peruuttamiseen apteekin julkisoikeudellisen aseman vuoksi, nostaa tapaus esiin tärkeitä käytännön kysymyksiä jokaiselle yritykselle, jolla on verkkosivusto tai verkkokauppa.

Tässä Dittmar & Indreniuksen markkinointi- ja tietosuojaosaajien nostot tapauksen opeista:

title: Meet our team at IBA Copenhagen
content:
We are looking forward to the upcoming International Bar Association’s Annual Conference 2026 in Copenhagen on 4 – 9 October. Our team is excited about meeting friends and colleagues from around the world, having engaging discussions and gaining valuable insights on global market trends.

Our Partners who will be attending the IBA Annual Conference would be delighted to meet you in Copenhagen during IBA week!

For any questions about our Silent Finnish Breakfast event or to book a meeting with our team, please contact our Director of Client Experience & Communications Katja Hollmén.

See you in Copenhagen!

Meet our team

“

Anders Carlberg, Senior Partner

“

Gabrielle Dannberg, Managing Partner

“

title: Simplification of the EU’s Corporate Sustainability Regulations – Where does Finland stand in summer 2026?
content:
The revision of the EU’s sustainability reporting and due diligence framework is underway in Finland in accordance with the EU’s Sustainability Omnibus Directive, which entered into force on 18 March 2026. The legislative revisions have significant implications for many Finnish companies, particularly by reducing the number of companies subject to reporting requirements. While many obligations have been eased, sustainability compliance remains firmly on the agenda.

Mario Draghi’s report on the future of European competitiveness, published in autumn 2024, triggered significant shifts in EU corporate sustainability regulation. These changes are becoming visible in 2026, particularly in the Corporate Sustainability Reporting Directive (“CSRD”) and the Corporate Sustainability Due Diligence Directive (“CSDDD”).
snippet: The revision of the EU’s sustainability reporting and due diligence framework is underway in Finland in accordance with the EU’s Sustainability Omnibus Directive, which entered into force on 18 March
creator: Elina Immonen-Seise

title: Finland‘s proposed expanded FDI regime: Key changes and takeaways
content:
Finland is in the process of enacting a new foreign direct investment (“FDI”) regime. On 3 June 2026, the Ministry of Economic Affairs and Employment (“MEAE”) published the draft government proposal for an Act on the Screening and Authorisation of Foreign Investments (“Proposed FDI Act”), proposing a significant expansion of and other major changes to the current regime. A new FDI Act is expected to enter into force in spring 2027. Once adopted, the number of cases subject to Finnish FDI review is expected to roughly triple, with an estimated 60–70 additional cases per year. In the following, our experts analyse the proposed key changes that investors and businesses should consider in transaction planning.

The Proposed FDI Act (sijoituslupalaki, in Finnish) would replace the existing Act on the Screening of Foreign Corporate Acquisitions (yritysostolaki, in Finnish), which provides the legal framework for the current Finnish FDI regime. The Finnish Government intends to revise the regime so that risks to national security, security of supply, and foreign influence activities are addressed more efficiently.
snippet: Finland is in the process of enacting a new foreign direct investment (“FDI”) regime. On 3 June 2026, the Ministry of Economic Affairs and Employment (“MEAE”) published the draft government proposal
creator: Rakel Markkanen
